text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain in the long run. For example, you might rename unclear variables, break long functions into smaller ones, or remove duplicate code. Refactoring doesn’t add new features or fix bugs—it focuses on the quality of the code itself. Over time, if code is not refactored, it can accumulate technical debt, meaning it becomes messy and harder to work with. Regularly refactoring helps teams avoid this, saving time and effort in the future.
